description: Record a failure-memory note for benchmark no-op false positives caused by weak task oracles.
prompt: Add a failure-memory record for a benchmark design bug path: `expected_files` is an allowlist in the runner, not a required-changes list, so a task can accidentally pass a no-op agent if its verification commands only check content that already exists. Create `docs/failures/0012-benchmark-noop-oracle-gap.md` using the existing failure-note structure. The note must explain the no-op false-positive risk, why it matters, the current replacement behavior, and name `tests/test_benchmark_tasks.py` plus `test_expected_files_are_required_by_task_oracles` as the concrete detection/prevention check. Do not edit scripts, tests, templates, benchmark task JSON, CI, or existing failure records.

commands.name: failure note oracle
command:
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
path = Path('docs/failures/0012-benchmark-noop-oracle-gap.md')
assert path.exists(), f'missing {path}'
text = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
required = [
    '# 0012.',
    '## Date Observed',
    '## Failure Type',
    '## Goal',
    '## What Happened Or Was Tried',
    '## Why It Failed',
    '## Current Replacement',
    '## Detection Or Prevention Check',
    '## Agent Guidance',
    '`expected_files`',
    'allowlist',
    'no-op',
    '`tests/test_benchmark_tasks.py`',
    '`test_expected_files_are_required_by_task_oracles`',
]
missing = [item for item in required if item not in text]
assert not missing, 'missing failure-note content: ' + ', '.join(missing)
status = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'status', '--porcelain=v1'], text=True)
changed = {line[3:] for line in status.splitlines() if len(line) >= 4}
assert str(path) in changed, f'{path} must be changed by this task'
